title: logFile until flag issue, negative duration replaced with positive
body:
we were adding a negative duration in podman events, causing inputs like
`-5s` to be correct and `5s` to be incorrect. Replaced the `ParseTime` line containing the negative duration with the positive one. Added tests.

fixes #11158

Signed-off-by: cdoern <cdoern@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body: @jwhonce is the test here incorrect due to the bug I am fixing? I feel like it should be length of 3 not length of 0
Would it make more sense to just take the input from the user and if it is a since just add a "-" to the front of it and then not change the interface to the function?  Or will this not work?
sure @rhatdan whichever you think is better
I would rather that then adding a new flag to make the same thing happen.  Seems simpler.

> Would it make more sense to just take the input from the user and if it is a since just add a "-" to the front of it and then not change the interface to the function? Or will this not work?

This should work but it should not be added when a user already set a minus because `--5s` would fail to parse.

Sure, in that case, I would remove the "-" in the since mode.

And I think the code already has that problem.

Actually, the caller cannot add anything to the string. It could also be a full unix timestamp, in that case nothing should be added. The caller has to little knowledge on the input, so the extra parameter works best IMO.
so @Luap99 @rhatdan are we going to leave as is?

> so @Luap99 @rhatdan are we going to leave as is?

I say yes.

/approve
/lgtm
I don't care enough to fight.
